why.1: The plan names exact repo paths: taskboard/storage.py, taskboard/routes/tasks.py, tests/test_tasks.py, and README.md. It covers both a tasks route/storage file and a tests/ file with non-approximate path strings.
why.2: Implementation steps 1–5 give concrete code changes but no per-step verification (no exact pytest target, no observable check after each step). Only a single end-of-plan Verification says `pytest tests/` — which the criterion explicitly rejects as insufficient.
why.3: Work is ordered into small steps that name code-level identifiers: storage task shape/due_date, _parse_due_date, create_task, update_task allow-list, list_overdue_tasks GET /overdue, backward-compat .get("due_date"), and named test functions. No vague units like 'update the backend'.
why.4: Tests are specified in tests/test_tasks.py with named functions and asserted behaviors: create with/without/invalid due_date, patch due_date, overdue excludes future/done/no-due-date, overdue only own tasks, overdue requires auth.
why.5: Codebase context anchors storage, routes, _auth, task shape, update_task allow-list, list_tasks behavior, and test patterns to file locations. Design/implementation state how due_date fits the existing task JSON shape in storage.py (null when absent, ISO string, optional, .get for old tasks).
why.6: No scheduler, queue, ORM, migrations, or new service; storage stays shape-agnostic JSON. Explicit Out of scope bounds sorting/pagination, timezones, cross-user overdue, and past-due validation on create/patch.

why.1: Names exact paths: `taskboard/routes/tasks.py` (implementation) and `tests/test_tasks.py` (tests); also references app/storage/users only as no-change.
why.2: Verification is only a terminal section (`pip install`, `pytest tests/`, manual curl). Steps 1–3 give no per-step command or observable check (e.g. import works, route responds).
why.3: Ordered steps: imports (`csv`/`io`/`Response`); `GET /tasks/export` / `export_tasks_csv`; filter by owner; columns; mimetype + Content-Disposition; empty CSV still 200.
why.4: `tests/test_tasks.py` adds three named tests: caller-only content, 401 unauthorized, empty header-only CSV with `id,title,done,notes`.
why.5: Background covers `X-Auth-Token` + `_auth` → 401; columns map id/title/done/notes and omit owner; ownership = token match.
why.6: Stdlib `csv` only; no new deps; out-of-scope bounds auth/storage/other endpoints, pagination/filtering, and README.

why.1: Names exact paths: taskboard/routes/tasks.py, taskboard/app.py, taskboard/storage.py, new taskboard/rate_limit.py, and tests/test_rate_limit.py (plus tests/test_tasks.py for fixture style). Touches both an app/routes file and a tests/ file as required.
why.2: Design steps 1–4 (new module, before_request wiring, create_app config, response contract) have no per-step runnable command or step-local observable check. Verification is only global: a Tests section plus one Manual verification block at the end (pytest/curl after everything).
why.3: Ordered, concrete units: in-memory fixed-window dict + threading.Lock in rate_limit.check; tasks_bp.before_request _enforce_rate_limit; create_app RATE_LIMIT_* config; 429 body/Retry-After shape. Mechanisms named, not vague phases.
why.4: Specifies tests/test_rate_limit.py with five concrete cases (per-token limit, independent tokens, invalid token, window reset, /users/ unaffected). Case 4 names test_allowed_again_after_window and monkeypatch of taskboard.rate_limit.time.time for deterministic window boundary.
why.5: States module-level _windows dict in taskboard/rate_limit.py, single-process rationale (app.py dev server, no workers), storage.py JSON reload pattern, no flask.g, why not Redis—file-anchored enough for a zero-context executor.
why.6: Forbids Redis/external stores; keeps only in-process limiting. Out of scope explicitly lists /users/ limiting, multi-worker, sliding/token-bucket, per-endpoint limits, remaining headers—no middleware package or proxy introduced.

why.1: The plan names taskboard/storage.py and both existing test files as tests/test_tasks.py and tests/test_users.py (plus routes, app.py, README). Paths match the required form; tests are named as leave-unchanged compatibility gates.
why.2: Verification is concentrated in Step 6 (pytest + smoke curl). Steps 1–5 have design/code/docs work without a per-step runnable command or concrete check for that step alone (e.g. no verify after schema/storage rewrite/default path/docs).
why.3: Work is ordered (schema → rewrite storage.py load/save/next_task_id → STORAGE_PATH default in app.py → optional JSON migration script → README → verify). Each step names the concrete interface or file, not vague units.
why.4: Keeps the load/save/next_task_id contract; states routes must not be refactored/changed; existing tests stay unmodified as the HTTP compatibility gate.
why.5: Gives full SQLite DDL (users/tasks columns mapped from the JSON shape) and a one-off migrate_json_to_sqlite.py with usage, or skip if no production JSON.
why.6: Uses only stdlib sqlite3; non-goals exclude race fixes, new endpoints, migration tooling as a permanent feature, pooling, route logic changes, ORM/frameworks.
